name: NanoBubbleEval-v1.0
description: An evidence-grounded benchmark for schema extraction, numerical grounding, and evidence attribution in the nanobubble and nanocarrier literature. The benchmark operationalises three failure modes of scientific information extraction (schema-fill hallucination, unit drift, cite-hallucination) as decomposed metrics over three task views projected from a single annotation pass.

creator.name: Anonymous Authors (NeurIPS 2026 Datasets and Benchmarks submission)

publisher.name: Anonymous Authors (NeurIPS 2026 Datasets and Benchmarks submission)

rai:dataCollection: Records were harvested from public scholarly APIs (PubMed/NCBI Entrez, Europe PMC, OpenAlex, CrossRef, Semantic Scholar, ClinicalTrials.gov v2) under five query families covering nanobubble core terminology, ultrasound and contrast imaging, delivery and release, biomedical nanocarriers, and environmental/agricultural applications. Deduplication is strictly ordered: DOI, then PMID/PMCID, then normalised title, then URL.
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: Some records lack abstracts (29.2% of the warehouse). The high-precision core and the gold pool are filtered to require an abstract; this constraint is documented in Appendix A of the paper.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: The raw API responses are not redistributed; the release ships the deduplicated warehouse manifest containing only fields permitted under the source-API terms of use.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Records are normalised to UTF-8, deduplicated in the order specified above, assigned tier labels A/B/C by lexical proximity to nanobubble-core vocabulary (Tier A is split A1/A2), and sampled into a 1,000-record balanced candidate pool. The 500-record gold pool is the top-ranked half of the candidate pool by an extraction-priority score; the gold-hard tier (n=40) is sampled with stratification on (nb_label, document_type) and pinned to the test split.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Annotations on the gold-hard tier are produced under a pre-specified blind, abstract-only protocol (Appendix C of the paper). Each annotation records the value, the canonical unit (for numeric fields), and a verbatim evidence quote substring of the abstract. The NOT_REPORTED sentinel is used when the abstract does not state a property. v1.0 ships single-annotator-validated labels under this protocol; full dual annotation is planned for v1.1.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: Inter-annotator agreement (Cohen's kappa on NOT_REPORTED-versus-emit, character-set span IoU on emit-emit pairs, unit-normalised numerical match on numeric fields) is planned for the v1.1 release; the v1.0 release ships first-annotator labels for downstream users to recompute agreement against their own annotators.
rai:dataAnnotationPlatform: Internal annotation tooling; no third-party crowdsourcing platform was used.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: The benchmark is versioned semantically. v1.0 is the abstract-only release with two-tier annotation. v1.1 is scheduled to add an open-access full-text gold subset, finalise multi-rater adjudication on the gold-hard tier, and add a same-family-larger ceiling baseline.
rai:dataLimitation: v1.0 evaluates abstract-derived extraction only; properties stated in tables, methods, or supplementary material are out of scope. The schema and metric design generalise but the lexical patterns and unit-normalisation tables are calibrated to the nanobubble domain. The gold-hard tier is single-annotator-validated under a pre-specified dual-annotator protocol; full dual-annotator IAA is planned for v1.1.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The benchmark exposes schema-fill hallucination, unit drift, and cite-hallucination as distinct failure modes in scientific information extraction, thereby guiding the development of more honest extraction systems. A foreseeable misuse is over-reliance on extracted property values for downstream scientific decisions; the abstention and evidence-provenance signals in the metric suite are designed to mitigate this.
rai:dataBiases: The warehouse is dominated by English-language abstracts indexed by the source APIs; this skews the benchmark towards English-language scientific literature. The cross-disciplinary balance reflects the relative volume of nanobubble research in biomedical, environmental, and fundamental physics venues.
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking schema-constrained extraction systems; benchmarking biomedical extractive-QA models; benchmarking instruction-tuned LLMs on numerical grounding under unit normalisation; evaluating evidence-attribution faithfulness in scientific NLP.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The benchmark contains structured metadata and abstract-derived annotations only; it does not contain identifiable medical records, patient-level information, or any personally identifiable data.

distribution.name: github-repository
distribution.description: Source code repository (Python package, pipeline scripts, and unit tests).

distribution.name: extraction_schema.json
distribution.description: 18-field schema specification with normalisation rules and missing-value sentinel (NOT_REPORTED).

distribution.name: numerical_grounding_benchmark.csv
distribution.description: Task 2 view: numeric value + canonical unit + tolerance.

distribution.name: evidence_attribution_benchmark.csv
distribution.description: Task 3 view: verbatim evidence span per emitted field.

distribution.name: splits.json
distribution.description: Deterministic dev/test split with five-slice tags.

distribution.name: leakage_report.md
distribution.description: Disjointness audit confirming no record_id overlap across splits.

recordSet.name: GoldPoolRecord
recordSet.description: One record per entry in the 500-record gold pool. Each record carries metadata (title, year, source, document type) and the 18-field schema annotation (six headline fields, twelve provisional fields).

field.name: abstract_or_summary
field.description: Abstract text used as the model input.

field.name: nanobubble_vs_nanoparticle
field.description: Slice label: nanobubble, nanoparticle, micro/nanobubble, or microbubble-adjacent.

field.name: size_value
field.description: Particle / bubble diameter (numeric headline field). Canonical unit: nm. NOT_REPORTED when absent in abstract.

field.name: zeta_potential_value
field.description: Surface charge (numeric headline field). Canonical unit: mV.

field.name: stability_value
field.description: Persistence / lifetime (numeric headline field). Canonical unit: h.

field.name: payload_value
field.description: Drug, dye, gene, gas, or cargo loaded (text headline field). Free text.

field.name: loading_efficiency_value
field.description: Encapsulation / loading percentage (numeric headline field). Canonical unit: %.

field.name: release_profile_value
field.description: Release behaviour (text headline field). Free text.
